role_summary: SNCC Mississippi project director from 1961; chief architect of the 1964 Freedom Summer; later founder of the Algebra Project (1982), which extended movement-organizing methods into mathematics education.

photo.alt: Bob Moses (right) with Julian Bond, "Fifty Years After Freedom Summer" event, February 2014
photo.photographer: Miller Center, University of Virginia (via Flickr)

ai_reading: The corpus discusses Moses across five transcripts: Timothy Jenkins (entry 120), Reginald Robinson (entry 101), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, the Sunflower County project director Moses oriented to voter-registration work), the John and Jean Rosenberg joint interview (entry 61), and Lawrence Guyot (entry 76, MFDP chairman). The cluster is the Mississippi-voter-registration-and-MFDP cohort, the network Moses built and ran across 1961-1964.
biographical_paragraph: Bob Moses is discussed in this corpus by 5 interviewees across the catalog (Timothy Jenkins (entry 120), Reginald Robinson (entry 101), Charles McLaurin (entry 17), John and Jean Rosenberg (entry 61), Lawrence Guyot (entry 76)), but was not interviewed for the Civil Rights History Project; this catalog page treats Bob Moses as an external figure whose presence in the archive is mediated through other voices. The canonical scholarly source the catalog cites for biographical orientation appears as [src: 1] below.

sources.title: Robert P. Moses and Charles E. Cobb Jr., Radical Equations: Civil Rights from Mississippi to the Algebra Project
